title: Lunar - Missions - Apollo 12 Mission
content: Commander Charles “Pete” Conrad and Lunar Module Pilot Alan Bean landed the Lunar Module Intrepid in the Ocean of Storms, a mere 535 feet from the robotic Surveyor 3 lander. On the second EVA, the crew explored the landing site out to a distance of 1300 feet from the lunar module. During the moon landing, Command Module Pilot Richard Gordon remained in lunar orbit in the Command Module, Yankee Clipper. Altogether, Apollo 12 spent 3.7 days in lunar orbit, circling the Moon 45 times. Apollo 12 Lunar Surface Journal EVA Transcripts. title: On Nov. 19, 1969, astronauts... - Arlington National Cemetery
content: ## Arlington National Cemetery's Post. Bean (Section 11) became the third and fourth men to walk on the moon. Their Apollo 12 mission had launched six days earlier, on Nov. 14, from Cape Kennedy, Florida, after a bolt of lightning struck their rocket as it left the launch pad. Along with gathering soil samples and surveying the surface, Apollo 12’s mission included deploying the Apollo Lunar Surface Experiments Package (ASLEP), retrieving pieces of the Surveyor III spacecraft, which had landed more than two years earlier, and setting up a TV camera. In orbit over the moon, Conrad separated the lunar lander from the command module, piloted by Dick Gordon (Section 18), and headed to the surface. During the return flight to the Command Module, Conrad let Bean take the lunar lander’s controls, something unheard of among astronauts. For their contributions to the Space Race, we honor the brave astronauts who explored the moon and safely returned to Earth.

title: Apollo 12 - Wikipedia
content: # Apollo 12. | Commander Pete Conrad studies the Surveyor 3 spacecraft, which had landed two years previously; the Apollo Lunar Module, *Intrepid*, can be seen at top right. | Spacecraft | * Command and service module CSM-108 * Lunar Module LM-6 |. | Apollo 12 crew Left to right: Conrad, Gordon, Bean Apollo program  ← Apollo 11  Apollo 13 → | |. **Apollo 12** (November 14–24, 1969) was the sixth crewed flight in the United States Apollo program and the second to land on the Moon. It was launched on November 14, 1969, by NASA from the Kennedy Space Center in Florida, landing on the part of the Moon called the Ocean of Storms on November 19, 1969. Apollo 12 would have attempted the first lunar landing had Apollo 11 failed, but after the success of the earlier mission, Apollo 12 was postponed by two months, and other Apollo missions also put on a more relaxed schedule.

title: Apollo 12 landing and EVA recreation (video)
